mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It is ideal for organizations looking to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Use the Oracle Cloud CLI to create a Kubernetes cluster. Replace placeholders with your actual values.

```bash
oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name <your_cluster_name> --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --kubernetes-version 1.30.1 --node-pool-configuration '[{"name":"nodepool1","nodeShape":"VM.Standard2.1","nodeShapeConfig":{"ocpus":1,"memoryInGBs":1},"initialNodeCount":3}]'
```

Ensure that you have the necessary IAM policies in place for your compartment.

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
After creating the cluster, configure kubectl to connect to it:

```bash
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region> --token-version 2.0.0
```

Verify the connection:
```bash
kubectl cluster-info
```

steps.title: Install OCI Cloud Controller Manager
steps.description:
Deploy the OCI Cloud Controller Manager using Helm. First, add the Oracle Helm repository:

```bash
helm repo add oracle https://oracle.github.io/oci-cloud-controller-manager
helm repo update
```

Then install the controller manager:
```bash
helm install oci-cloud-controller-manager oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager --namespace kube-system --set provider-config=provider-config-example.yaml
```

Ensure the controller is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling for your node pool:

```bash
oci ce node-pool update --node-pool-id <your_node_pool_id> --management-config '{"autoScale":true,"minNodes":1,"maxNodes":5}'
```

You can also set up monitoring using Oracle Cloud Monitoring services.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the OKE cluster, use the following command:

```bash
oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force
```

This will remove the cluster and associated resources. Ensure to clean up any remaining resources in your compartment.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubernetes Version
upgrade.description:
To upgrade your OKE cluster to a newer version, first check the current version:

```bash
oci ce cluster get --cluster-id <your_cluster_id>
```

Then, initiate the upgrade:
```bash
oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version 1.30.1
```

Before upgrading, ensure you have backed up any critical data. After the upgrade, verify the cluster status:
```bash
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: kubectl not connecting to the cluster
troubleshooting.description:
If kubectl cannot connect, ensure your kubeconfig is correctly set up. Check the context:
```bash
kubectl config current-context
```
If necessary, regenerate the kubeconfig using:
```bash
oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region> --token-version 2.0.0
```

troubleshooting.title: OCI Cloud Controller Manager not running
troubleshooting.description:
If the OCI Cloud Controller Manager pod is not running, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -n kube-system <pod_name>
```
Ensure that the provider configuration file is correctly set up and that the necessary IAM policies are in place.

troubleshooting.title: Node pool scaling issues
troubleshooting.description:
If nodes are not scaling as expected, check the node pool configuration:
```bash
oci ce node-pool get --node-pool-id <your_node_pool_id>
```
Ensure that the min and max nodes are set correctly and that there are no resource constraints in your compartment.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new node shapes and improved performance metrics.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of older Kubernetes versions below 1.28.

versionNotes.changes: Enhanced security features and added support for additional OCI regions.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed support for legacy authentication methods.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of OKE includes a running Kubernetes cluster, configured kubectl access, and the OCI Cloud Controller Manager deployed and operational. You should be able to manage your workloads seamlessly on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add oracle https://oracle.github.io/oci-cloud-controller-manager
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the OCI CLI installed and configured with the necessary permissions.
